# Preface {.unnumbered}

**EB-Lab** ([Estela Blanco](https://estelablanco.cl/), together with the [ClimChange-NewbornHealth](https://github.com/ClimChange-NewbornHealth) GitHub organization) focuses on **environmental exposure and perinatal health** in Chile and comparable settings. This manual summarizes how we collaborate, version analyses, and publish code so work stays reviewable and reproducible.

::: {.callout-note}
## Living document
The manual evolves. If anything disagrees with the [official contributor guidelines](https://github.com/ClimChange-NewbornHealth/Lineamientos_colaboradores), follow that repository and suggest an update here via **Source** in the sidebar.
:::

It draws inspiration from the [Benjamin-Chung lab manual](https://jadebc.github.io/lab-manual/) and modular resources such as Yale DSDE’s [*Book of Workshops*](https://ysph-dsde.github.io/Book-of-Workshops/).

## How to use this manual {.unnumbered}

Each chapter is a quick decision or checklist before `git push`:

- Who should read it and when.
- Minimum standards before independent work.
- Links to guidelines and reference repositories.

For the Spanish edition, use **Español** in the top bar.

## Scope {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-important}
## Limits
This manual **does not replace** institutional protocols (ethics, consent, administrative confidentiality, etc.). It **does set minimum technical standards**: clear `README`, MIT license, large data off-repo, relative paths.
:::
